text: The proliferation of artificial intelligence in creative industries has sparked a nuanced debate about authorship and originality. Proponents argue that AI augments human capability, enabling artists to explore previously inaccessible realms of expression. Critics, however, contend that the delegation of creative decisions to algorithms risks homogenizing artistic output and undermining the deeply personal nature of creative work. Ultimately, the most compelling outcomes arise when AI serves as a collaborator rather than a replacement, preserving the intentionality that distinguishes meaningful art from mere production.
